title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Poll heat rises in Palakkad as BJP trains guns on UDF MLA scandal
content: The BJP has fielded Sobha Surendran, who helped the party leap to the second position in the constituency for the first time in 2016, hoping to wrest the seat from the United Democratic Front. The Left Democratic Front has been finishing in third spot in the segment for the past three elections. Neither the UDF nor the LDF has announced candidates for the segment

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Political crossovers gather pace in Kerala as Assembly elections near
content: Defections and cross-affiliations are blurring the traditional ideological boundaries between the United Democratic Front, Left Democratic Front, and the National Democratic Alliance. Increasingly, electoral pragmatism rather than ideological loyalty appears to be shaping political choices

title: Amrutanjan possessed Mylapore Kapaleeswarar Temple land till 2018, has ₹9.74 crore rental arrears: HR&CE dept. tells Madras High Court

title: SC declines to intervene in Calcutta High Court order for NIA probe into Beldanga violence
content: On February 11, the apex court questioned the invocation of the UAPA; on March 16,the court finds the High Court’s confirmation of the NIA probe into the case ‘quite balanced’

title: All five NDA candidates win Rajya Sabha polls in Bihar, including CM Nitish Kumar and Nitin Nabin
content: Three MLAs of Congress party and one MLA of Rashtriya Janata Dal (RJD) skipped the voting. RJD MLA Kumar Sarvjeet alleged that BJP used the money power to win the election.

title: AAP govt. fulfilled all pre-poll promises in just 4 years: Punjab CM
content: Mann says key promises include free electricity, 65,000 govt. jobs and 5.5 lakh employment opportunities; Oppn. calls it ‘most disappointing govt.’

title: BJP announces first list of candidates for 144 seats for Bengal Assembly polls

title: NDA wins nine Rajya Sabha seats from Bihar, Odisha, Haryana
content: BJP national president Nitin Nabin and Bihar CM Nitish Kumar romp home as NDA secures all five Rajya Sabha seats in Bihar; the party also secures victory of three candidates in Odisha; BJP and the Congress file separate plaints in Haryana

title: Thoothukudi schoolgirl’s murder: HC directs Vilathikulam DSP to take up probe
content: A Division Bench of Justices N. Sathish Kumar and M. Jothiraman directed the Vilathikulam DSP to record audio-video statements of the parents of the girl, and asked the parents to cooperate with the investigation

title: Hardeep Puri’s daughter moves Delhi HC to remove content linking her to Jeffrey Epstein
content: In her lawsuit seeking ₹10 crore in damages, Himayani Puri has also called for an order to restrain several entities from disseminating defamatory content; it is likely to be heard in the HC next week

title: Congress, DMK hold seat-sharing talks for the second time
content: During the first round of talks held on Saturday last both parties stuck to their stand of leading the INDIA bloc in the U.T. for the 2026 elections

title: Supreme Court says stakeholder consultation needed, reluctant to alter duration of five-year law courses
content: While acknowledging that reforms in legal education are a necessity, the Chief Justice-led Bench says it may not be appropriate for courts to determine the duration of professional courses.

content: The U.S. Commission on International Religious Freedom is ‘presenting a distorted and selective picture of India’, the Ministry of External Affairs says

title: Naval warships escort Indian vessels from the Gulf of Oman

title: M.P. High Court Bench to visit Bhojshala-Kamal Maula mosque site before April 2 hearing
content: Under an agreement with the ASI in 2003, Hindus are allowed to perform puja at the complex every Tuesday, while Muslims offer namaz there every Friday.

content: The company allegedly supplied adulterated dairy products by substituting milk fat with palm oil and other harmful chemicals

title: Five convicted in 2016 Una flogging case; Gujarat court to announce punishment

title: Government clears 23 institutions to set up ‘quantum labs’

title: Stranded in Doha, Chennai resident reaches Riyadh by road to fly back home

content: The External Affairs Minister was invited to interact with Foreign Ministers from the 27-member bloc, during the EU Foreign Affairs Council meeting, says a statement from Ministry of External Affairs

title: 1.12 lakh acres donated to Waqf Board, but it currently controls only 20,054 acres: Zameer
content: The Board lost 23,627 acres following implementation of the land reforms, says Housing, Waqf and Minority Welfare Minister B.Z. Zameer Ahmed Khan; he reiterates that Hindu temples have not encroached upon any Waqf Board properties.

title: Pension issue of aided educational institutions’ staff  will be referred to committee: Madhu Bangarappa
content: BJP member S.V. Sankanur urged the government to consider pension benefits for employees of aided educational institutions who were appointed prior to 2006 and later brought under the grant-in-aid system, as well as those appointed after 2006.

title: Maternity, childcare leave for women teachers to be sanctioned within 15 days
content: Women teachers returning to duty after maternity leave can seek a transfer to a place of their choice during counselling, says School Education and Literacy Minister Madhu Bangarappa; this facility will be available until the child completes five years.

title: SSC exams begin in Andhra Pradesh, 98.90% attendance recorded on Day 1

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: In rapidly urbanising Kozhikode North, a closer electoral contest looms
content: Home to several key administrative institutions and educational establishments, the constituency reflects the city’s evolving urban character and complex electoral dynamics. It comprises 32 divisions of the Kozhikode Corporation and is characterised by a sizeable upper- and middle-class voter base.

title: State universities suffering from ‘serious chronic ailments’, need long-term treatment: M.C. Sudhakar
content: Raising the issue under Rule 330, BJP member Talwar Sabanna said State universities had become centres of problems, alleging that they were plagued by caste-based issues and corruption and that their overall functioning had deteriorated.

title: B.Ed admission fee revision to be taken up from this academic year
content: Higher Education Minister M.C. Sudhakar said that the admission fee for B.Ed. colleges in the State had not been revised for the past 26 years.

title: As 10,796 remain untraced, Karnataka High Court directs police to effectively implement SOP for handling complaints of missing persons
content: The court has now specifically asked the State government to file a report on establishment of district missing persons units, quarterly review report of supervisory committees, and digital portal for tracking of missing persons, among others

title: New paramedical admission norms may trigger educational crisis, closure of colleges: Sharan Prakash Patil
content: Medical Education Minister Sharan Prakash Patil said the Commission had stipulated that only students who have passed II PUC in the science stream would be eligible for admission to paramedical courses. The rule has created apprehension among institutions in the State.

title: Man Jumps To Death From 23rd Floor With 2-Year-Old Daughter In Gurugram
content: A private school operator allegedly jumped from the 23rd floor of a residential building with his two-year-old daughter in his arms here on Monday, killing both, police said.

title: 19-Year-Old Dental Student Arrested For Links With ISIS Online Module In UP
content: The Uttar Pradesh Anti-Terrorism Squad (ATS) has arrested a 19-year-old student pursuing dentistry for allegedly having links with an online module of the banned terror outfit ISIS, officials said on Monday.

title: Om Birla To Move Motion To Revoke Suspension Of 8 Opposition MPs Today
content: The suspension of eight opposition MPs from the Lok Sabha is likely to be revoked on Tuesday, sources said, citing a decision taken at a meeting convened by Speaker Om Birla.

title: Not Gold, Not US Dollar, This Asset Has Surged The Most During Iran War
content: Since the war has triggered a sell-off of equities (considered riskier), investors are scrambling to park their money in safe-haven assets.

title: Pakistan-Afghanistan Conflict Live: Afghan Cricketers React To Pakistani Attack On Kabul Hospital That Killed 400
content: Pakistan-Afghanistan Conflict Live Updates: At least 400 people have been killed after Pakistani airstrike hit a hospital in Afghanistan's capital Kabul, Taliban spokesman said.

title: 7-Year Jail, Heavy Fines: Maharashtra Passes Freedom Of Religion Bill
content: The Maharashtra assembly on Monday night passed the Freedom of Religion Bill 2026, which has stringent provisions to prohibit religious conversions carried out through coercion, fraud, inducement or marriage, by voice vote.

title: Delhi Records Cleanest Air Since October 2025, All Pollution Curbs Lifted
content: All restrictions imposed in Delhi-NCR under the Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) have been revoked after a significant improvement in air quality, officials said on Monday.

title: Video Shows Large Explosion After Pakistan Airstrike On Kabul Hospital
content: An Afghan Taliban spokesperson said that 400 people were killed and 250 injured in the strike on a Kabul hospital.

title: With 400 Deaths, Pak Bombing On Kabul Hospital 'Deadliest' In Afghan History
content: Hundreds of people were reportedly killed or injured at a drug treatment centre in Afghanistan's capital, Kabul, after an airstrike that the Taliban government blamed on Pakistan.

title: US Urges Allies To Designate Iran's Military, Hezbollah As Terrorist Groups
content: President Donald Trump's administration on Monday urged US diplomats abroad to push allies to designate Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps and Lebanon-based Hezbollah as terrorist groups.

title: Fareed Zakaria On 2 Lessons China Took Home In Seeing US Go To War Again
content: The images from the war in the Middle East are being watched carefully in Beijing not with alarm, but with calculation.

title: "I Could Do Anything": Trump Says He Will Have "Honour Of Taking Cuba"
content: US President Donald Trump vowed Monday to "take" Cuba as the communist island plunged into darkness under a total power blackout linked to a crippling oil embargo imposed by Washington.

title: Trump Says Iran Could "Blow Up The Entire Middle East" If It Gets Nukes
content: Trump said preventing Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons was essential for global security and argued that the country would use such weapons immediately if it possessed them.

title: Amid Death Rumours, Netanyahu's Message For "Brave People Of Iran"
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu sent wishes to Iranians Monday for the upcoming Nowruz holidays, marking the Persian new year, as his country presses its war on Iran.

title: Israel doesn’t seek ‘perpetual war’ with Iran, says Envoy, suggests diplomatic options remain
content: e-Paper The View From India Looking at World Affairs from the Indian perspective . first day first show news and reviews from the world of cinema and streaming . the weekly newsletter from science writers takes the jargon out of science and puts the fun in!

title: Taliban accuses Pak of bombing drug rehab centre; Islamabad says military infra hit — 10 key points
content: Pakistan airstrikes have struck a drug rehabilitation hospital in Kabul, Afghanistan. Hundreds have died and many more are injured. Afghan officials accuse Pakistan of deliberately targeting civilians. Pakistan denies this, stating strikes hit militant infrastructure. Cross-border clashes continue, raising regional security concerns. Both nations present conflicting casualty figures.

title: Video: Blazing C-RAM shreds Iranian drones, rockets targeting US Baghdad embassy
content: The Counter-Rocket, Artillery and Mortar (C-RAM) system is built to shield military bases, embassies and critical infrastructure from incoming rockets, artillery shells and mortar fire. Developed by the US Army during the Iraq war,  a rapid-fire platform was originally designed to intercept missiles. Its land-based variant was adapted to tackle short-range threats typical of insurgent attacks.

title: 'I stand with my people': Rashid Khan on Pakistan airstrikes in Kabul
content: Afghanistan's cricket star Rashid Khan has condemned alleged Pakistani airstrikes in Kabul, which reportedly killed over 400 people and destroyed a drug rehabilitation hospital. Khan called the targeting of civilian sites a war crime and urged the UN to investigate. Pakistan denies intentionally hitting civilian areas, stating strikes targeted militants.

title: Moody’s Mark Zandi on Block layoffs: If firms cut jobs like this, losses likely
content: Moody's Analytics chief economist Mark Zandi warns of AI's growing impact on the labor market following Block's job cuts, suggesting automation is a key driver. He notes that increased AI adoption is linked to weaker hiring, and if more companies follow suit, outright job losses are likely.

title: 'Principles of Dravidian ideology and Islam are fundamentally the same': Stalin Jr.
content: Tamil Nadu deputy chief minister Udhayanidhi Stalin, who aslo happens to be the son of chief minister MK Stalin, said no "conspiracies" can break the relationship between the urling DMK and the Muslim coummunity. "As long as brotherhood and unity exist, no matter how many attempts or conspiracies take place, no one will be able to break the relationship between the DMK and the Muslim community," said Stalin, who is DMK's heir apparent.

title: ‘Iran crossed every red line’: Gulf nations’ message to US amid Middle East tensions
content: Gulf Arab nations, while not seeking a US-led war with Iran, warn that inaction risks Iran threatening vital oil routes.  US officials are pressing for regional support for a military campaign, as Iran's attacks have heightened fears of it holding the region's energy supply hostage. Gulf leaders believe decisive action is needed to degrade Iran's military power.

title: How Sanju Samson created a 'spiritual place' in dressing room after WI win
content: Following India's T20 World Cup triumph, Sanju Samson reflects on the spiritual victory and overcoming self-doubt, highlighting a crucial innings against the West Indies. He also shares an emotional moment with coach Gautam Gambhir and looks forward to his new chapter with Chennai Super Kings, aiming to inspire young cricketers.

title: Nvidia CEO seemingly 'realises' that Google, Microsoft and Meta are set to eat company's lunch
content: Nvidia's dominance in AI hardware is facing a significant challenge as customers seek cheaper, purpose-built inference chips.  Major tech companies like Google, Microsoft, and Meta are developing their own alternatives, directly competing with Nvidia's established GPU dominance. This shift signals the end of Jensen Huang's 'one chip fits all' era.

title: 'Fabricating tales of Islamophobia': India slams Pak at UN; cites Kabul strikes during Ramadan
content: India has voiced strong concerns at the UN regarding the dangerous trend of weaponizing religious identity for political gain by both state and non-state actors.  Ambassador Parvathaneni Harish highlighted atrocities in India's western neighborhood, criticizing fabricated Islamophobia claims and brutal repression of minorities. He asserted that Muslims in India have elected representatives and enjoy peaceful coexistence.

title: Stock market today: Nifty50 opens flat; BSE Sensex down 50 points
content: Stock market today: Nifty50 and BSE Sensex opened flat in trade on Tuesday after a day of gains. While Nifty50 was near 23,400, BSE Sensex was down around 50 points.

title: FASTag annual pass gets pricier: Here's what changes from April 1
content: The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has increased the FASTag Annual Pass fee to Rs 3,075, effective April 1, 2026. Introduced in June 2025 and rolled out in August last year, the pass offers unlimited toll crossings for non-commercial private vehicles for a year. The revision aligns with the National Highways Fee Rules, 2008.

title: Watch: Flames, huge smoke columns seen after Pakistan airstrike on Kabul hospital that killed 400
content: Pakistan launched Operation Ghazab lil-Haq, conducting strikes on Afghan Taliban military installations in Kabul and Nangarhar Province. The operation Pakistan said targeted technical support facilities, ammunition storage, and drone assembly workshops, with Pakistan stating the action followed unprovoked firing. Afghan authorities have accused Islamabad of hitting a hospital resulting in death of over 400 civilians.

title: Oil price today: Crude continues to climb amid Hormuz supply crisis; Brent, WTI jump 2%
content: Middle East conflict intensifies, driving oil prices higher as fears of supply disruptions mount. The Strait of Hormuz remains largely disrupted, impacting production and leading to calls for strategic reserve releases. Analysts warn of further price hikes if tensions persist, potentially impacting global economies.

title: 400 killed, 250 wounded in Pakistani airstrikes on Kabul hospital, Taliban claims
content: Afghanistan accused Pakistan of killing civilians in fresh airstrikes on Kabul, alleging a hospital treating drug addicts was hit. Pakistan denied targeting civilians, stating precision strikes hit military and "terrorist" targets used against Pakistan. Tensions escalate amid ongoing border clashes and accusations of sheltering militants.

title: NFL Trade Rumors: Cowboys could move $6M star WR despite franchise tag talk
content: Trade speculation continues around a star Cowboys receiver despite the team placing the franchise tag on him for the 2026 season. Some analysts suggest Dallas could trade him to avoid a contract holdout, possibly for a second-round pick. However, many believe that price is far too low given his recent production, making a deal unlikely for now.

title: Los Angeles Lakers vs Houston Rockets injury report: Who's in, out for March 16 game
content: Los Angeles Lakers face the Houston Rockets in a crucial Western Conference clash on March 16, 2026. Both teams are in strong form, with the Lakers on a five-game winning streak and the Rockets formidable at home. Key players like Luka Dončić and Kevin Durant are expected to feature in what promises to be a tightly contested battle.

title: Phoenix Suns vs Celtics injury report: Key players out, stats & H2H
content: The Phoenix Suns face the Boston Celtics on March 16, 2026, with Phoenix battling frontcourt injuries. Boston, boasting a strong defense and rebounding, has most players available. Key players like Devin Booker for Phoenix and Jayson Tatum for Boston will be crucial as the Suns navigate their Western Conference challenges against the Eastern Conference leaders.

title: 'I must have made mistakes': Gambhir opens up on Rohit, Kohli equation
content: Head coach Gautam Gambhir acknowledged potential missteps in his interactions with senior players Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma. While not directly addressing retirement speculations, Gambhir stated that mistakes are human and his decisions were always made with good intentions. Despite some criticism, his tenure has seen significant trophy wins for India.

title: 'Deducted Rs 4,000 for leave': Man shoots bank manager dead in cabin
content: A Punjab & Sind Bank guard allegedly shot and killed the branch manager in Ghaziabad after a heated argument over leave and salary issues. The incident occurred in the manager's cabin in front of staff and customers. The accused, a former Army personnel, was later arrested after being found drinking.

title: Bengaluru man beaten to death with cricket bat for refusing hafta; 7 held
content: A mechanic was brutally beaten to death by a group with a cricket bat and logs near Wilson Garden, allegedly over protection money demands and a dispute concerning his shop's concrete roof construction. Seven individuals have been arrested following the violent incident that occurred in front of the victim's children. Police are investigating the exact circumstances.

title: UP ATS arrests ISIS-linked BDS student for running online radicalisation module
content: Uttar Pradesh ATS arrested a 19-year-old BDS student for allegedly radicalizing youth and recruiting for ISIS through social media and encrypted platforms. The student, Harish Ali, operated anonymous accounts, spread propaganda, and maintained contact with foreign handlers, aiming to establish a Sharia-based Caliphate.

title: 'I try to focus': Vaibhav Sooryavanshi reveals how he handles 'attention'
content: Young prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, 14, is navigating the pressures of IPL stardom, focusing on his game amidst rising popularity. His career highlight remains a match-defining 175 off 80 balls in the 2026 U-19 World Cup final, a performance that secured India's sixth title and is cherished above his IPL century.

title: RS elections: NDA sweeps Bihar, bags three in Odisha; Haryana result put on hold
content: The NDA has significantly bolstered its Rajya Sabha presence, securing all five seats in Bihar and three of four in Odisha. Haryana's results are pending due to a vote secrecy controversy involving Congress MLAs. This round of elections saw the NDA win eight of eleven contested seats, strengthening its position in the Upper House.

title: ‘Nobody has seen him’: Trump casts doubt on Mojtaba Khamenei’s survival amid Iran war
content: US President Donald Trump has expressed significant doubt regarding the health and even the survival of Iran's newly appointed Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei. Citing conflicting reports of severe injuries, including a lost leg and disfigurement, Trump noted the unusual lack of public confirmation of Khamenei's well-being, fueling widespread speculation about his condition and whereabouts.

title: Celina Jaitly reacts as brother refuses to contact, wants to consult wife
content: Celina Jaitly has reacted after the Delhi High Court dismissed her petition seeking consular access, legal assistance and communication with her brother, Major (Retd.) Vikrant Jaitly, who is currently detained in the United Arab Emirates. The court disposed of the plea after being informed that Vikrant Jaitly did not wish to communicate with his sister and preferred to handle legal matters only after consulting his wife, Charul Jaitly.

title: No cricket talk: MS Dhoni had this advice for CSK's Rs 14.2 crore recruit Prashant Veer
content: Chennai Super Kings' new recruits, Kartik Sharma and Prashant Veer, were signed for Rs 14.2 crore each.  Prashant received financial advice from MS Dhoni, not cricket tips.  The young all-rounder is eager to learn and adapt to the IPL environment, focusing on his own game rather than comparisons to established players.

title: Businessman Kevin O'Leary wears card featuring Jordan, Kobe, LeBron at Oscars
content: "Shark Tank" investor Kevin O'Leary wears the 2004 Upper Deck Exquisite All NBA Access Pass . it features game-used NBA logo patches worn by Michael Jordan, leBron James and Kobe Bryant . he also wore a custom-made Dolce & Gabbana jacket that took seven months .

title: Man jumps to death from 23rd floor with 2-year-old daughter in Gurgaon
content: A 38-year-old man tragically jumped from the 23rd floor of a residential tower in Gurgaon, taking his two-year-old daughter with him. The incident occurred on Monday morning, with both declared dead on arrival at a hospital. The man had reportedly been suffering from health issues, including vision loss, since the Covid pandemic.

title: Rajpal Yadav on slapping Akshay Kumar in Bhooth Bangla
content: Actor Rajpal Yadav recently opened up about a scene in Bhooth Bangla where he slaps Akshay Kumar, a moment that reminded fans of the iconic scene in Bhool Bhulaiyaa where Akshay’s character slapped his popular character Chhota Pandit. When asked if the moment could be seen as a playful form of revenge, Rajpal reacted with excitement and teased the audience about the scene.

title: 'Distorted picture of India': MEA slams US report recommending sanctions on RSS, R&AW
content: India has strongly rejected the US Commission on International Religious Freedom's 2026 report, calling it biased and distorted. The MEA spokesperson criticized the commission's reliance on questionable sources and ideological narratives, urging it to address religious freedom issues within the US. India maintains its commitment to religious harmony.

title: Taylor Swift faces fresh fan criticism during Travis Kelce’s big career week
content: Taylor Swift has seen her public approval dip in a recent poll, sparking renewed debate about her chart strategies and the timing of several music releases. Critics questioned moves tied to her albums The Tortured Poets Department and The Life of a Showgirl, including a video drop that coincided with Bad Bunny’s Super Bowl appearance. At the same time, Travis Kelce confirmed he will return for a 14th season with the Kansas City Chiefs, explaining that his competitive drive and unfinished goals pushed him to keep playing.

title: Happy Eid-ul-Fitr 2026: 51 inspiring Eid Mubarak wishes and messages
content: As Ramadan 2026 concludes, Eid-ul-Fitr approaches with a blend of spiritual reflection and joyous celebration. Expected around March 20-21, the festival offers a chance to reset, mend relationships, and reconnect with loved ones. The article provides a collection of heartfelt wishes for family, friends, and partners to spread love and cheer during this special occasion.

title: England all-rounder blasts Brendon McCullum, reveals 'an eye-opening experience'
content: Liam Livingstone criticised England management, including coach Brendon McCullum and director Rob Key, for poor communication after he was dropped in 2025. The all-rounder said the experience was “eye-opening” and expressed frustration over his role and batting position. Now with Sunrisers Hyderabad for the Indian Premier League 2026, he hopes strong performances revive his England chances.

He currently tracks the energy sector, writing on power, renewable energy, coal and mines. title: After 9-day voyage, India-flagged tanker arrives from Qatar, 2 more to reach today
content: Indian-flagged tanker, Shivalik, departed from Ras Laffan port in Qatar carrying 46,000 tonnes of LPG, arrived at Mundra port in Gujarat on Monday . the ship is carrying 81,000 tonnes of Murban crude, a crude grade produced by the UAE .
